summary: Solid 1976 show with standout Scarlet Begonias and a magical Dancin'‑>Wharf Rat‑>Dancin' sequence, captured on a mixed audience/soundboard matrix that’s good but not pristine.
review: Fans rave about the extended Scarlet Begonias and the seamless Dancin'‑>Wharf Rat‑>Dancin' jam, plus strong renditions of Music Never Stopped, Peggy‑O and Looks Like Rain. The recording is a matrix of audience tape for the first set and Cotsman soundboard for the second, offering clear inner details but with occasional EQ quirks and a cut on Around & Around.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise the musical highlights; recording is good but has minor issues, leading to an overall strong yet not exceptional rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First set is audience (AUD) only, second set and encore are soundboard (SBD) with some patches; inner detail is clear, but listeners note a need for EQ adjustments and a 44‑second cut on Around & Around.

standout_songs: Dancin' in the Streets > Wharf Rat > Dancin'
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set (AUD) is considered less engaging, while the short second set (SBD) shines with the Dancin'‑>Wharf Rat‑>Dancin' jam; overall a lower‑tier 1976 performance but still enjoyable.

band_member_comments.Phil: Described as 'possessed' on this night, indicating an especially inspired bass performance.
